claimText: Tulare Lake is located in the San Joaquin Valley of California.
evidenceExcerpt: Tulare Lake: San Joaquin Valley, California
examUse: Correct-pair matching for lakes and their geographical locations.

claimText: The Aral Sea is located on the boundary between Kazakhstan to the north and Uzbekistan to the south.
evidenceExcerpt: The Aral Sea is located on the boundary between Kazakhstan (North) and Uzbekistan (South).
examUse: Map-based boundary questions or correct-pair matching for water bodies.

claimText: The Congo River is the deepest river in the world and crosses the equator twice.
evidenceExcerpt: The Congo River crosses the equator twice, is the deepest river in the world
examUse: Statement-based questions on unique geographical features of world rivers.
distractorHooks: Crosses the Tropic of Cancer twice

claimText: The Congo River basin holds the world's largest tropical peatland.
evidenceExcerpt: its basin holds the world's largest tropical peatland.
examUse: Environment and geography statement-based questions regarding carbon sinks and peatlands.

claimText: The Blue Nile originates from Lake Tana in Ethiopia.
evidenceExcerpt: The Blue Nile originates from Lake Tana in Ethiopia

claimText: The Blue Nile meets the White Nile at Khartoum to form the Nile River.

claimText: The Great Ruaha River is a major tributary of the Rufiji River in Tanzania.

claimText: The Seine River is the second longest river in France after the Loire River and empties into the English Channel.
evidenceExcerpt: Seine River: Second longest river in France after Loire... empties into the English Channel at Le Havre.
